A couple more I have done per an emergency responder friend:
1) Remove all outside hoses from the house. These can become projectiles in high wind & if enough force could damage the pipe
2) Latch the garage door from the inside (latches are on the door) Most don’t even know the latches are there. This may keep the door from flying up in extreme winds
3) If you have pets, prepare a place in the garage for them to use (kiddy pool with pee pads or a piece of artificial turf)
4) If you have an iPhone install the latest update. This will allow satellite use in case cell service is down
Stay safe everyone! These updates are so appreciated

I freeze gallon and quart sized ziplocks to use as blocks of ice. They stay frozen for a long time, much longer than cubed ice. The quart side is also good for dipping a cloth in to keep cool when the electricity goes out.

Great list! A couple of our tips to add:
- The fuller a freezer is, the longer to thaw. Fill open spaces with containers of water to freeze (bottled water is good for this but kids may be tempted to pull the frozen water out when the power is out, door needs to stay closed.)
- Garbage cans of water for flushing: Absolutely essential but is a serious drowning hazard for children, so secure the lid if you can (temporarily duct-tape shut, etc).
- Don’t ignore boil-water notices. You don’t want to be heaving up your hurricane supplies for days in the hot sun so that you won’t use up your flushing water.

When you say unplug electronics, you’re referring to TVs, etc? Or are you referring to fridges & appliances? I feel like I should know this, but I just want to confirm.

Keep in mind just because your electronics are “powered off” the power source is still active and on “standby”. A power fluctuation, as in a drop in voltage or a spike will fry the power source. Also turn OFF your water heater. A “brown out” drop in voltage” can burn up the heater. As voltage decreases, amperage increases, burning up equipment just a surely as an over voltage.
You can have no power. (Dead service) get a lightning strike to the power lines and it will fry everything still connected. Unplug everything AND THROW the main service breaker to off.
